explicit_gaps.description: The corpus defines retention classes, expiry basis, and erasure blockers, but does not publish one canonical per-artifact duration matrix.
Threat classes are normatively defined in prose in the runtime hardening contract, but no dedicated schema-backed enum exists in the current corpus.
The corpus forbids stored authority login credentials by design, so there is no dedicated persisted schema artifact for that credential class.
Restore privacy reconciliation states are defined, but the corpus does not separately publish a per-surface reopen matrix for each operator and customer route family.

rows.encryption_or_protection_control: server-side validation, anti-CSRF or device binding, and explicit revocation audit
rows.erasure_behavior: Session invalidation rotates or revokes challenge state and blocks replay of stale commands.
rows.legal_hold_behavior: Session audit lineage survives logout, compromise response, or administrator invalidation.
notes: Browser, native, and automation sessions share one machine-readable contract.
rows.survivability_or_fidelity_formula: not_formula_based__every_command_validated_against_current_actor_session_state

rows.encryption_or_protection_control: durable command_id, request_hash, idempotency_key, and authoritative object refs
rows.erasure_behavior: Expired success projections keep the same recovery-anchor family instead of degrading into transport-only timeout markers.
rows.legal_hold_behavior: Receipts preserve command recovery anchors and session lineage during investigations or incident review.
notes: Durable receipts are the northbound recovery anchor after lost POST responses or app relaunch.
rows.survivability_or_fidelity_formula: duplicate submits collapse onto one durable receipt and one legal state transition.

rows.encryption_or_protection_control: schema-validated lifecycle state plus canonical retention-tag alignment
rows.erasure_behavior: PSEUDONYMISED and ERASED states require durable request/action/proof linkage.
rows.legal_hold_behavior: LEGAL_HOLD and ERASURE_PENDING states require concrete follow-up workflow refs and next checkpoints.
notes: Lifecycle-owned follow-up refs must not leak into terminal-only states.
rows.survivability_or_fidelity_formula: lifecycle posture participates directly in RETENTION_EVIDENCE_GATE and downstream blocking effects.

rows.encryption_or_protection_control: append-only audit evidence with minimized sensitive payloads
rows.erasure_behavior: Payload bodies may expire, but object, reason, lineage, and audit-family minimums must survive.
rows.legal_hold_behavior: Audit evidence remains durably queryable during legal hold and release transitions.
notes: Audit events survive payload expiry as the proof-of-record boundary.
rows.survivability_or_fidelity_formula: audit-sufficiency policy requires object, reason, and lineage minimum after payload expiry.

rows.canonical_store_or_boundary: authenticated authority ingress checkpoint and inbox
rows.encryption_or_protection_control: authenticated provider channel, dedupe key, and request-lineage binding
rows.erasure_behavior: Ingress authentication and lineage proof may be minimized, but cannot be reconstructed from transient transport memory.
rows.legal_hold_behavior: Authority ingress proof survives legal hold because it anchors request-lineage and duplicate suppression.
notes: Legal-state mutation is blocked until authenticated ingress, dedupe, and request-lineage correlation complete.
rows.survivability_or_fidelity_formula: not_formula_based__authority_ingress_authentication_and_lineage_proof_must_survive_transport_retries

rows.encryption_or_protection_control: binding-lineage refs plus request hash and idempotency lineage
rows.erasure_behavior: Deletion is blocked where it would obscure already observed authority history.
rows.legal_hold_behavior: Authority-facing history ambiguity cannot be resolved by deletion and must remain explicit.
notes: Authority history ambiguity is reconciled, never erased.
rows.survivability_or_fidelity_formula: authority-state reconciliation outranks any attempt to erase or silently rewind already observed legal state.

rows.canonical_store_or_boundary: shared cache identity contract serialized on read models and native scenes
rows.encryption_or_protection_control: exact security-context match, delivery revalidation, and temporary artifact purge policy
rows.erasure_behavior: Broader variants purge immediately on access or masking narrowing and must not silently survive.
rows.legal_hold_behavior: Hold does not widen cache reuse; the contract only controls lawful reuse and purge semantics.
notes: Cache identity is a security boundary rather than a performance-only concern.
rows.survivability_or_fidelity_formula: reject reuse on context, route, projection-version, or preview mismatch.

rows.canonical_store_or_boundary: client portal read model and visibility cache boundary
rows.encryption_or_protection_control: cache_isolation_contract plus externalization_governance_contract.delivery_binding_hash
rows.erasure_behavior: Background refresh, stale tabs, or direct URLs cannot widen the reviewed slice once masking or route context drifts.
rows.legal_hold_behavior: Portal reuse remains bound to customer-safe projection and visibility posture even under retention limitation.
notes: Portal caches are always customer-safe and must stay exact to client, route, and visibility partition.
rows.survivability_or_fidelity_formula: projection_information_ratio(o) and delivery_binding_hash bound the customer-safe slice.

rows.canonical_store_or_boundary: governed upload session and staging store
rows.encryption_or_protection_control: frozen request identity plus checksum/scanner and binding posture
rows.erasure_behavior: Staged bytes remain non-downloadable and cannot satisfy a rebased request silently.
rows.legal_hold_behavior: Follow-on hold or erasure decisions must preserve staged lineage and publish posture explicitly.
notes: Binary transfer is the governed exception to the general command surface.
rows.survivability_or_fidelity_formula: not_formula_based__stale_bytes_never_satisfy_current_request

rows.encryption_or_protection_control: upload session lineage, visibility binding, and malware-scan gated publication
rows.erasure_behavior: Quarantined or policy-limited files render as typed unavailable state rather than disappearing silently.
rows.legal_hold_behavior: Published attachment lineage and unavailable reasons remain visible during hold or quarantine review.
notes: A customer-visible attachment may appear as pending placeholder but not as downloadable content before scan clearance.
rows.survivability_or_fidelity_formula: not_formula_based__customer-visible_downloadability_requires_clean_scan_and_available_publication_state

rows.encryption_or_protection_control: integrity-sealed frozen config with durable hash lineage
rows.erasure_behavior: If direct payload retention narrows, explicit limitation placeholders must preserve the frozen execution basis.
rows.legal_hold_behavior: Legal hold blocks erasure when the config freeze remains necessary for proof preservation.
notes: ConfigFreeze belongs to the preserved basis set for historical replay.
rows.survivability_or_fidelity_formula: Replay-safe basis retention; if unavailable, surface RETENTION_LIMITED rather than silently swapping newer configuration.

rows.encryption_or_protection_control: masked projection policy plus stable ref/hash lineage
rows.erasure_behavior: Visible confidence and explainability must degrade rather than overclaim when retained support narrows.
rows.legal_hold_behavior: Underlying legal hold preserves the truth basis that informs the bundle's visible limitation posture.
notes: DecisionBundle is a disposable read model and cannot replace authoritative truth after restore or replay.
rows.survivability_or_fidelity_formula: projection_fidelity(o) degrades user-visible certainty relative to retained decision-side confidence; masking must never overstate certainty.

rows.encryption_or_protection_control: masked export policy with explicit omission_entries[]
rows.erasure_behavior: Exports must keep retention binding, omission entries, and limitation notes under retention pressure.
rows.legal_hold_behavior: Legal hold and omission posture must travel with the export when relevant.
notes: An enquiry export must remain honest about lawful omissions.
rows.survivability_or_fidelity_formula: projection_fidelity(o) may narrow exported visibility, but omission and limitation disclosure must remain explicit.

rows.encryption_or_protection_control: append-only proof lineage with workflow and audit refs
rows.erasure_behavior: Erasure can never destroy the append-only proof that request, decision basis, and execution outcome occurred.
rows.legal_hold_behavior: Erasure proof must show when legal hold blocked or released the workflow.
notes: Erasure proof is durable even when the erased content is not.
rows.survivability_or_fidelity_formula: not_formula_based__proof_of_erasure_must_remain_durable

rows.encryption_or_protection_control: retention-limited explainability contract with typed omission semantics
rows.erasure_behavior: Expired or erased edges must remain explicit present-but-limited evidence, not broken graph absence.
rows.legal_hold_behavior: If legal hold blocks erasure, the graph must show the retained-limited posture rather than hiding it.
notes: EvidenceGraph cannot render retention limitation as a missing edge.
rows.survivability_or_fidelity_formula: limitation_explicitness(o) must remain typed and non-zero wherever support narrows.

rows.encryption_or_protection_control: sealed ordered gate tape and audit lineage
rows.erasure_behavior: Derived gate lineage may survive with explicit limitation notes even when underlying support narrows.
rows.legal_hold_behavior: Gate lineage remains on hold when needed to preserve decisive-path audibility.
notes: Retention-driven gate changes must also emit correlated error and audit objects.
rows.survivability_or_fidelity_formula: Decisive path continuity must keep silent_ambiguity(o) = 0 or progression blocks.

rows.canonical_store_or_boundary: hash set and basis lineage store
rows.encryption_or_protection_control: sealed hash lineage with append-only provenance
rows.erasure_behavior: Hashes survive payload minimization and may outlive direct content as the lawful replay placeholder.
rows.legal_hold_behavior: Any hold preserving replay basis must preserve execution-basis hash lineage.
notes: Hash lineage is preserved even when human-readable payload detail is no longer retained.
rows.survivability_or_fidelity_formula: execution_basis hash continuity is mandatory even when payload artifacts degrade to RETENTION_LIMITED posture.

rows.encryption_or_protection_control: per-tenant envelope encryption and explicit limitation/tombstone semantics
rows.erasure_behavior: Eligible content may be minimized or pseudonymised, but the engine must preserve hashes, lineage, and limitation notes.
rows.legal_hold_behavior: Legal hold preserves auditable input lineage while keeping any erasure blocker explicit.
notes: Authoritative intake basis hashes must remain queryable even when payload content is minimized.
rows.survivability_or_fidelity_formula: survivability(o) and limitation_explicitness(o) govern whether the frozen input remains filing-capable, review-only, or tombstoned.

rows.encryption_or_protection_control: delivery_binding_hash plus masked export policy and typed omission entries
rows.erasure_behavior: Exports inherit masking/export policy and may not bypass it through direct object-store URLs.
rows.legal_hold_behavior: Export limitation posture stays explicit if hold or minimization narrows the delivered slice.
notes: Masked exports prefer summaries, masked fields, and lawful omission rather than raw payload copies.
rows.survivability_or_fidelity_formula: projection_information_ratio(o) must never exceed decision_information_ratio(o).

rows.canonical_store_or_boundary: native hydration legality envelope on cursors and scenes
rows.encryption_or_protection_control: schema compatibility, projection guard, session lineage, and purge inventory
rows.erasure_behavior: Purges regulated local artifacts on tenant, session, masking, route, preview, or schema drift.
rows.legal_hold_behavior: Hold does not bypass first-paint legality or live rebase requirements.
notes: Native speed may reuse compatible state but may not outrun legality.
rows.survivability_or_fidelity_formula: verify compatibility before render or restore; no mutation after cache-only restore until live legality returns.

rows.canonical_store_or_boundary: device-local structured cache, resume metadata, preview cache, temp export, local search index
rows.encryption_or_protection_control: OS-protected storage plus shared purge policy and route/object binding
rows.erasure_behavior: NSUserActivity, preview caches, temporary exports, and search indices are purged or invalidated with cache state.
rows.legal_hold_behavior: Local derivatives purge with the same revocation and masking boundaries as structured cache.
notes: Desktop-local derivatives inherit the same masking/export posture as the underlying server object.
rows.source_heading_or_logical_block: 11. Security and runtime posture for the desktop client
rows.survivability_or_fidelity_formula: local_artifact_purge_policy requires purge with cache state on revocation, masking change, or schema incompatibility.

rows.encryption_or_protection_control: retention_binding plus limitation_notes and explicit support posture
rows.erasure_behavior: A filing-capable bundle must downgrade when decisive support falls below τ_submit or any decisive limitation is silent.
rows.legal_hold_behavior: Hold may preserve proof-path support while keeping hold lineage explicit on the retained bundle.
notes: ProofBundle is retained explainability, not a viewer cache.
Silent proof degradation is forbidden.
rows.survivability_or_fidelity_formula: controlling proof only when survivability(o) >= τ_submit and silent_ambiguity(o) = 0; otherwise downgrade to review/audit posture with limitation notes.

rows.encryption_or_protection_control: candidate-bound comparison evidence and basis hash lineage
rows.erasure_behavior: If supporting payload narrows, replay must surface RETENTION_LIMITED explicitly instead of synthesizing a newer basis.
rows.legal_hold_behavior: Replay attestation persists when required to prove historical comparison outcome.
notes: Replay attestation is part of the preserved historical comparison trail.
rows.survivability_or_fidelity_formula: Replay basis remains lawful only when preserved artifacts or placeholders keep deterministic comparison honest.

rows.encryption_or_protection_control: checkpoint, restore drill, audit continuity, replay limitation, and compensating re-erasure lineage
rows.erasure_behavior: Restore-time resurrected erased data opens compensating re-erasure workflow before reopen.
rows.legal_hold_behavior: Blocked legal hold, proof preservation, and authority ambiguity states remain explicit legal posture, not notes.
notes: Restore evidence is not lawful production evidence until privacy reconciliation reaches a reconciled state.
rows.survivability_or_fidelity_formula: reopen_access_state remains BLOCKED or LIMITED until privacy reconciliation reaches a reconciled state.

rows.encryption_or_protection_control: schema-validated control object with durable basis refs
rows.erasure_behavior: Carries erasure_eligibility, erasure_reason_codes, limitation behavior, proof preservation basis, and authority ambiguity blockers.
rows.legal_hold_behavior: Canonical legal-hold state, hold ref, and change chronology live here.
notes: The tag carries effective expiry and blocker refs rather than delegating recomputation downstream.
rows.survivability_or_fidelity_formula: tag drives downstream retention_evidence gate and fail-closed erasure posture.

rows.encryption_or_protection_control: per-tenant envelope encryption plus append-only manifest sealing
rows.erasure_behavior: Payload minimization may remove direct content, but manifest lineage and RETENTION_LIMITED placeholders must survive without rewriting basis hashes.
rows.legal_hold_behavior: Legal hold may block erasure and must remain visibly queryable via the canonical retention tag.
notes: RunManifest is part of the minimum lawful replay basis.
Retained history must never silently collapse into not applicable.
rows.survivability_or_fidelity_formula: survivability(o) >= τ_audit for tombstone continuity; controlling proof requires survivability(o) >= τ_submit and silent_ambiguity(o) = 0.

rows.canonical_store_or_boundary: token vault / secret store metadata boundary
rows.encryption_or_protection_control: versioned secret lineage, KMS/HSM rooted master keys, and attestation refs
rows.erasure_behavior: Rotation fails closed if attestation or version binding is unknown; retired and revoked chronology remains serialized.
rows.legal_hold_behavior: Secret rotation chronology remains auditable even across retirement or revocation.
notes: No secret lineage may self-supersede or invert attestation chronology.
rows.survivability_or_fidelity_formula: not_formula_based__attested_rotation_cutover_and_historical_read_window_must_remain_monotonic

rows.encryption_or_protection_control: request-lineage, token-binding, and reconciliation proof
rows.erasure_behavior: Restore or queue recovery cannot blindly replay mutations from transport artifacts alone.
rows.legal_hold_behavior: Submission history remains preserved when required for legal-state audit and replay-safe recovery.
notes: Queue recovery is transport rebuild, not truth replay.
rows.survivability_or_fidelity_formula: authority mutations require lineage and binding revalidation before resend or reopen.

rows.canonical_store_or_boundary: observability pipeline and audit-adjacent log store
rows.encryption_or_protection_control: redaction, masked values, stable refs, and audit-vs-telemetry separation
rows.erasure_behavior: Diagnostic logging must not reintroduce personal or authority-secret data that policy would otherwise hide.
rows.legal_hold_behavior: Logs and traces preserve correlation lineage but remain subordinate to audit proof.
notes: Telemetry omits high-volume detail when needed, but cannot remove mandatory audit history.
rows.survivability_or_fidelity_formula: not_formula_based__telemetry_never_becomes_proof_of_record_for_erasure_or_hold

rows.encryption_or_protection_control: frozen binding scope hash and typed rebinding state
rows.erasure_behavior: Rebases and supersession surface as typed states rather than silent rebinding.
rows.legal_hold_behavior: Binding posture survives so upload rebase decisions remain auditable during legal or retention review.
notes: Upload rebinding is explicit and machine-readable.
rows.survivability_or_fidelity_formula: resume existing session only; stale bytes never satisfy current request.

rows.canonical_store_or_boundary: read model store plus cache partition
rows.encryption_or_protection_control: cache_isolation_contract and visibility cache partition key
rows.erasure_behavior: Caches rebuild from durable truth; broader variants purge immediately on visibility or masking narrowing.
rows.legal_hold_behavior: Snapshot visibility remains subordinate to upstream truth and retention posture.
notes: Inbox caches must remain visibility-scoped and never widen across staff/customer boundaries.
rows.survivability_or_fidelity_formula: projection_fidelity(o) and visibility partition constrain what may be reused.

rows.canonical_store_or_boundary: workspace snapshot store and route-stable stream spine
rows.encryption_or_protection_control: shell stability token, access binding, masking fingerprint, cache partition key
rows.erasure_behavior: Cached workspace posture is rebuildable from durable truth and must rebase rather than infer missed transitions.
rows.legal_hold_behavior: Workspace reads surface retention-limited or quarantined state explicitly rather than masking it as absence.
notes: Clients recover through resume or rebase, never by recreating hidden transitions locally.
rows.survivability_or_fidelity_formula: projection_fidelity(o) narrows visible certainty; stream_recovery_contract governs replay-safe continuity.
